Sites are large and in great location to beaches, parks, bike tracks and the strand.
Would have been ideal if the reception staff did not allocate us a camper van size site for a 9m caravan. The size of the van was explained both on the phone while booking and again in the office upon arrival,  where we were assured there would be plenty of room. There wasn't,  however we were allocated another site fairly quickly. Too bad if it had been fully booked when we arrived. It is a busy park.
Cold showers in rear ladies amenities block both times that I used the showers. One late in the evening and the other in the afternoon. There are 3 laundries with only 2 working driers over the whole park, which are at a premium for this large park.  1 at rear laundry was broken.
Boom gate broken.
The park is very expensive at around $70 per night.
Grounds and gardens ver pretty. Toilets clean.

Nice park.  Plenty of trees. Showers were cold too often. No hot water.  We expect hot showers no matter what we are paying per night - and when paying a premium rate a cold shower is unacceptable.  Not the only Tasman park with a hot water problem in 2025.  Pleasant reception staff were clueless when advising the best approach to caravan sites - and when it was later pointed out (very diplomatically) to them - they seemed disinterested.

The place is fine, but the cleanliness of the common areas is not the best. The kitchen is full of flies, and they don't control the guests who leave everything in a mess.
The large kitchen has a very small refrigerator, and the small kitchen has a very large refrigerator.
The internal paths need repairs.
Security is not the best; at night, people who appear to be under the influence of drugs come in, shouting and going into the bathrooms.
The administration staff are very disrespectful and unfriendly; only one of them treated us kindly.

El lugar esta bien pero la limpieza de los espacios comunes no es la mejor, la cocina esta llena de moscas, no controlan a los huspedes que dejan todo hecho un asco.
La cocina grande tiene una heladera muy pequeña, y la cocina pequeña tiene una heladera muy grande.
Los caminos internos necesitan unas refacciones.
La seguridad no es la mejor, de noche se mete gente que parece estar bajo los efectos de las drogas a gritar y meterse en los baños.
Los de la administración son muy irrespetuosos y faltos de amabilidad, solo una de ellos nos trato de manera amable.

Stayed for 12 nights. Good park with decent sized sites. Some have been updated with fake grass and concrete slabs. Reception staff nice and they sell a few basic grocery items. Pool was ridiculously cold, but I believe  is getting some solar heating in the near future which I think more parks should consider for the winter months. (Park gossip lol) large and clean amenities, with codes to enter and small handles (ladies) to open and a larger one would be so much easier. Camp kitchen well appointed, but 2 of the bbqs didn't work. Bus stop across road and shops within a 10minute walk. Will return.

Only one downside and that's the distance to the amenities - but that does depend on where you're located.

Beautiful grounds, spotless amenities and large clean indoor kitchen. Being across the road from the waterfront is great for a pooch walk.

Definitely stay here again!
Nice caravan park opposite the beach. Good clean amenities. Lots of trees for shade.

We recently stayed at this park. We were allotted site 21 in the non powered section at the back of the park as there were no powered sites available. In my opinion the only difference in these sites should be the lack of power and water. The sites were VERY uneven, in long grass and very near the dump point and the rubbishy part of the park.
In fact the whole park was very much in need of some water, lawn mowing, tree pruning and green waste cleanup. The amenities were average and the clothes line area was overgrown and covered in litter. We paid premium prices for sites that we wouldn’t have chosen even at a free camp. And got eaten by sandflies. Such a shame. It has great location and has the potential to be amazing.

Stayed for 5 days, The camp kitchen were not cleaned to an expected standard the cleaners came every second day the bins were overflowing, power points didn’t work, bbq didn’t work. The disable toilet was locked apparently they only had 1 key so couldn’t keep it open and it was the only one I could use as I had 2 broken arms. Staff appeared not interested - far too expensive for what’s there.

We have stayed here before 3 years ago because we love the location.The amenities were very tired and not that clean the roads around the caravan park needed some attention very rough and pot holes in places especially after rain.The park in general seem to lack care and attention .Considering the cost of $100 per night just to plug into power and maybe not use the amenities  pool not even slightly heated .We have stayed at a lot of places for half the price and twice the facilities.Come on Rowes Bay make a better effort.

We recently stayed at Rowes Bay BIG4 Caravan Park and honestly, it was one of the best holiday park experiences we’ve ever had. From the moment we arrived, we were greeted with genuine warmth and hospitality, and it set the tone for our entire stay.

The location is unbeatable—right across from the beach with stunning views of Magnetic Island, and just a short drive into the heart of Townsville. The grounds were immaculate, the facilities clean and modern, and everything was beautifully maintained. You can really tell the team takes pride in the park.

What made our stay truly exceptional, though, was the manager. They went above and beyond to ensure we were comfortable and well taken care of. Whether it was offering local recommendations, helping us settle in, or just checking in to make sure we had everything we needed, their attentiveness and kindness did not go unnoticed. It’s rare to feel this looked after on holiday—it honestly felt like a home away from home.

Whether you’re passing through or planning a longer stay, we can’t recommend Rowes Bay BIG4 enough. We’ll definitely be back!

We stayed in the park on a powered van site. It was a tidy park, very busy comings and goings. The site C1 we had was nice except the kids playground was right behind it. I would think the sites around the playground would be better suited to families and not couples with no kids. I don't really want to be sat around a kids park trying to relax with screaming kids around me. Hence we are going to a child free park in mission beach. Maybe a bit more consideration as to where they put the guests.

After reading the shocking recent reviews I was terrified of checking in. Wondering whether they stayed at the same place because we had no issues. We were a powered campervan on site E17 was a bit tricky to get up on the verge but we got there eventually. We were inbetween an amenities block and a camp kitchen. Toilets and showers were always clean and everything worked. Showers are nice and big with a lot of space to hang things and shelving which is a nice change. There were empty laundry machines every time I went past. Camp kitchen could do with a second hob as there was a queue when we were using it which makes you feel bad. Theres no utensils in the kitchen so take all your own stuff. Really nice walk across the front into Townsville I recommend getting up for sunrise!

We stayed a week here and found the park had quite a few issues. The ammenities were unclean and had broken tap/soap/ shower fittings. The site we were allocated was quite close to and within full view of the RV dump point which was pretty disgusting to see so many people emptying their waste morning noon and night. At times is was quite smelly. We also found out that the park owners appear to use a dynamic pricing model so no one (not even the staff) knows what each site cost will be on any given day - it’s all over the place and makes you feel likes it’s price gouging. Also, at around 2am on one night, there was some sort of fight between two guys in the ammenities block - heaps of yelling, foul language. The fight lasted for about an hour and I was concerned enough to want to call the park security/emergency number. However, the contact number is noted as being posted on the reception office door - which at 2am and with an out of control fight going on , was no place we could safely get to. This park needs much better management and quality control. Do not recommend staying here.

We were in a powered caravan site
We booked months in advance and we explained we needed a large site on booking
When we arrived van fitted fine no where to park car so we were told to park it out on the street
Shower waste blocked up and amenities not very clean , toilets not flushing , we booked for 4 night and had to move sites for the last night and there were empty sites right next to us , very badly managed , park is in a good location but that’s it for me , I won’t be going back

Stayed in a so called  Deluxe cabin. It was bad, mouldy shower, holes in walls, tiny bedroom, cooker was hardly usable. Cabin looks really old and tired, not worth the high prices they are charging.  Disappointing stay.
Sauberer Platz, gute Lage! Bushaltestelle vor der Tür,  hinten kein Straßenlärm auf den Stellplätzen für Wohnmobile.

We unfortunately had the mixer in our shower leaking/pouring water our entire stay. It was reported, but nothing done to fix an easy problem. It made getting to sleep each night difficult, due to the sound of constant running water, even with the door shut. Great location however and amazing birdlife. We would of enjoyed our stay more if the problem had been rectified
Way way way too expensive we got elsewhere for half the price, rip off seriously

It’s too expensive for camping 3 people with a tent in unpowered site is 108$
We booked only because we got really stuck
The facilities is nice but still don’t worth the overpriced site
Stayed in a standard cabin for one night, For the level of accom provided it was well overpriced at $239.00,  a motel would have been far better value.
Amenties clean , Grounds immaculate, Very friendly helpful staff. Will definitely stay again
Stayed here travelling with my partner on our trip up north and thought it was great, staff are super accommodating. Great atmosphere and fair priced! 10/10 recommend

Beautiful park to stay and visit. Had a ball with the kids in the crystal clear pool. Amenities were clean and very up to date.
Camp kitchen was spotless.
Plenty of grassed area to kick the footy, play cricket with the kids.
Also staff were very welcoming and great to get along with.
We will be back for sure

Great little spot !
Found the manager to be most helpful on our stay.
What a wonderful experience had there.
Will definitely be back.

This is a great location right accross from the nth section of the esplanade.  Coffee van in the morning,  pizza van in the evening if you don't feel like cooking.
Amenities very clean, as well as a very tidy park. I have stayed in my campervan here a few times now and the staff have always been very helpful and friendly.  This is my go to spot in Townsville.
Good spot to explore Townsville ,nice view of magnetic Island 🏝 from across the road 👌very nice camp kitchen👍

Big4 Tasman Holiday Parks – Rowes Bay is a large and busy park that’s well-equipped with all the amenities you’d expect. It made for a great base to explore everything Townsville has to offer. The facilities were clean and well-maintained, and despite how busy the park was, it still felt organised and comfortable.

One of the best features is the location – directly across from Rowes Bay with access to parks, walking trails, and beautiful views. It’s also just a short Uber ride into the city, making it easy to enjoy both nature and urban attractions. A convenient and enjoyable spot for families or travellers wanting to be close to it all.
Staff were great cabins were clean bonus for me was the icecream sold at reception

Very disappointing, the caravan site with the artificial grass loaded with midgies - the minute you walked on it you were bitten up to your knees.  Everyone around us complained. Regular spraying by management would help.
They get first prize for the thinnest toilet paper of all sites we've been toin 15 years.  Paper not usable unless folded 6 times and impossible to get from dispenser without ripping and littering all the cubicle floors.  Their procurement department should visit some NRMA caravan sites to see the difference in their paper quality. Either that or use some of Tasmans at their own homes - they would very quickly change supplier.
The park is in an excellent location but just needs some detailed  housekeeping from management

We checked the price an hour before we arrived and were unable to book on-line. At arrival the price was almost 50% higher than quoted because 'we have only two sites left'. An appalling policy in which the supply/demand principle over-rides decency. We do not recommend. At another, equally suitable park the service was extremely pleasant, the price much less, and we were supplied a powered site for the cost  of unpowered. Guess who gets no stars and who gets five.....

Amazing campground! Stayed on a powered site with our campervan and had a great Overall experience. Facilities are quite new and very clean. Showers are warm and the best I had so far on a campsite. Very close to a Beautiful beachwalk:) loved it and would come back all the time.

Had the worst experience here!
- unable to access room because of roadworks inside the park
- no hot water inside the room st kitchenette
- ants throughout the room
- hot water ran out at showers at 7pm
- boom gate to exit property did not work
- dirty towels from previous guests left in the room
Will say the location on the beach is beautiful but does not make up for the quality of the accommodation. Very disappointing
Will be back!  Great place to stay
Power and water connection easy. Bathroom facilities clean. Communal cooking clean
Camping in a caravan very easy to park. My husband was very impressed did it first time.
Really good little botanical garden topped off with a big avairy containing rare finches etc. Also got first shots of the beautiful Sun birds.
Great location, beautiful environment,  pool, tennis courts, helpful staff

If you have a Big Rig and medium/large dogs then avoid this place like the plague.
For you are not welcome and that will be made clear to you.  Also do not book through Wiki Camps, the information there is incorrect.
The reception staff were argumentative

What can I add to this review?
RULES, RULES and MORE RULES yet the Park is an OH&S nightmare.
Don’t do this, don’t do that, it’s a wonder anyone would want to stay here.

1.	❌ They insisted that only small breeds of dogs were allowed into the park, yet we saw a number of border collies that were being walked (which was in breach of their Rules).
2.	❌ There is No dog exercise yard on site.

3.	❌ They put our Large Rig next to a most unfriendly, surly couple in a Camper Trailer. Obviously not dog lovers, they gave us foul looks right from the get go.

4.	❌ We received a visit from, what we can only assume, was the Park Manageress. She is a nasty piece of work.
No hi, how are you, enjoying the park type banter. Nope it was straight to the point…. There have been complaints that you have not cleaned up after your dogs…. (a blatant lie).
That we had consistently left our dogs unattended and that they barked and whined continuously ….. (another blatant lie)
At which point the conversation went downhill rapidly. We denied the accusations that were made, but would she listen to the truth. You guessed it. No she would not.
All the time waving a piece of paper about the rules that we had agreed to.
We maintained that there was no truth in the complaints and that she should inform the complainer of that fact.
She finally said that they could ‘make’ us go, if anymore complaints were made.
At no stage was she subjected to either verbal or physical abuse.
The thing that really upsets me is that she immediately took the side of the complainers and anything we said was irrelevant.

5.	❌ There are so many trip hazards around the park, steps without the yellow safety strips, speed bumps damaged,  No  edge warning strips on the concrete pads, Water points with metal stakes angled out and nothing to wam anyone of danger..There are sewer traps on raised concrete slabs with metal entry covers, and No yellow warning stripes. One of which I tripped over on our first night in when walking the dog.. I injured my knee, but when I later reported it at Reception, the girl there was not interested in writing down the details, just told me that it had to be submitted via email. No, how are you,  no kiss my arse, no nothing. Absolutely no concern that I had suffered an injury due to their lack of compliance with OH&S regs.

6.	❌ The markup on their drinks is off the planet with 750ml bottles of soft drinks costing $8. each. (Bloody highway robbery)

Will we ever return? ………..Not in this lifetime ❌
Do we recommend this place?…. You are joking, Right! ❌

If I could,  I would give this place a Minus 5 Star rating

Good location and that's all. Run down facilties, garbage containers full of maggots, no soap and no toilet paper, customer unfriendly (assistant manager doesn't serve waiting customers because she is busy training a new employee)...not in line with other big4 standards
This place is awesome, i highly recommend.

Loved the cabins, and location of park. just the shower puts out a lot of water all over the floor even with shower door closed. And I feel there could be a curtain rod and curtain to separate the main bed from the kitchen and bunks. Just a thought.. would give some privacy getting out of small shower.
Overall enjoyed our stay.
Location was just a short ride to the Strand. Good clean facilities 👍

Reception & Staff very friendly and helpful. Very affordable and value for money. Everything was clean and tidy. Amenities were great and clean. Local Managers which means great knowledge of the area and things to do. Will definately be returning! Thanks for a great stay.
Very nice park. The Villa cabins are small but very clean,comfortable and updated. Was a very welcome reception.
Beautiful location by the beach and near The Strand. Lovely park area. Very friendly and helpful staff. Pool area is lovely.

Excellent location,nice caravan spots great staff only to happy to help upgraded us for free to a drive through spot as it was raining left 2 days early as the cyclone and floods were coming otherwise would have stayed even longer close to bars and foreshore

Terrible campsite, the kitchen and bathrooms were dirty. The campsite was completely neglected with roads covered in grass and leaves and parking spaces for vehicles left to themselves. The worst thing is that I did not feel safe at all, with people camping in the campsite probably living there permanently with dogs without a leash and who bothered us.

Tasman holiday park located in rows bay is a great holiday get away with great fun for adults and kiddos. The rooms are well maintained and cleaned for all guests. The rooms had fresh ice, cold water and milk. The rooms also had stocked up with other supplies we needed. Rows bay holiday park is stacked with staff who well trained, and are really nice. Durning our  we encountered a problem with our water. This problem was resolved almost instantly. During our stay we found that is located only 5 minutes away from the strand. In the maintained office there is cheap ice cream on offer, drinks and snacks.
Thank you staff of rows bay for the amazing stay 👝🦛❤️🧸

Die Stellplätze sind teilweise recht ordentlich, allerdings etwas ungepflegt.
Die sanitären Einrichtungen sind zwar recht ordentlich, aber es wird offensichtlich nicht gereinigt. Als wir ankamen, lagen Haare an einem Waschbecken und auf dem Boden, von einer „Wildsau von Camper“ der sich hier die Haare geschnitten hat. Am nächsten Mittag waren sie immer noch da. An der Rezeption reklamierte ich die fehlende Reinigung. Man entschuldigte sich zwar, aber bis zur unserer Abreise am nächsten Tag, wurde immer noch nicht gereinigt.
Ich kann den CP nicht empfehlen, da ich auf Grund der mangelnden Hygiene hier meine Bedenken habe.

10.2.2025
Wir sind seit fast 4 Wochen mit dem Wohnmobil unterwegs und haben noch nie so einen verschmutzten Platz gesehen.

Waschraum habe ich hier ein Video gepostet, das man für so etwas noch Geld verlangt, aber leider mussten wir waschen. Die Waschmaschine wäscht nicht richtig so das die Wäsche muffig und immer noch dreckig raus kommt. Einmal waschen kostet 6 $ und trocknen genauso 6$.

Damen Badezimmer… mir fehlen die Worte es zu beschreiben… eine Schande!

Wir benutzen hier nichts und benutzen unsere Bad im Wohnmobil. Da auf unsere Route (Cairns) Hochwasser gibt müssen wir hier noch  bis morgen früh bleiben.

Es wird auch nicht kontrolliert wie und wo man parkt. Wir hatten einen „Nachbar“ der sich falsch hingestellt hat, somit konnte er direkt bei uns auf dem Teller gucken. Siehe Bild.

Location location!
Stayed with my wife in a cabin last week. From the moment we drove in and met the lovely lad at the reception till the morning we left. We couldn’t speak highly enough of the staff they were always willing to lend a hand as I needed to change a tyre on my 4wd. Ryan was only to happy to help. Him and his partner’s knowledge of the local area was fantastic and put us on to some fantastic local eateries. The park is kept neat and tidy and always seen Ryan out and about emptying the bins and cleaning the camp kitchen. The park isn’t a resort in the Whitsundays but it’s clean tidy reasonably priced and the Ryan his partner and the staff were friendly and helpful. We will be back again.

Dreadful and dirty. Stayed early Jan 25. Hired a cabin and travelled with friends who stayed in a tent. Do not come here, cabins are filthy and I mean filthy. Manager only interested in smoking cigarettes rather than assisting with costumers and acknowledging how dirty our cabin was. The camp kitchen open all night so food stolen and had homeless using as a place to sleep. Could not find any staff in evening to assist with there being no gas in camp kitchens bbqs. Amenities and site were not cleaned or maintained over the weekend and bins were overflowing by Sunday evening. Shame as I stayed here a year ago and managers before would go out of their way to help. Tasman sought this out as the other parks I have stayed at including rolling stone and mission beach are beautiful. This campsite is no where on par with your other sites.

Extremely disappointed with our stay, leaving a day early. The room was musty and stank of stale cigarettes and urine. We were issued with damp linen that also matched the smell of the room. The only ablutions available were inaccessible due to flooding and there were no alternatives. We found drug paraphernalia in the cupboards in the room, indicating a thorough clean had not been conducted between guests.
Extremely disappointed, and a waste of money.

We had been looking forward to staying here a for a while. Front counter service by checkin was good.

The room was nice and cold with the aircon having been turned on for us by the staff prior to our arrival.

The room smelt like the cleaners had been smoking.

There were ants everywhere and I mean everywhere. We went to Coles to get some spray and had to spray the counter top, the ceiling, the floor, beside the bed, the table, the bathroom. This was a daily routine. Went to wash our hands but there was no little soap provided like you get everywhere else in Australia. Someone had pinched the plug in the kitchen and also a couple of the forks.
When the staff member came with the plug I told him about the ants everywhere and the fridge door shelf that was missing as well as the supplied shampoo and body wash pump bottles in the shower being empty and the conditioner bottle just having some water in it. Also asked if there was any dishwashing liquid. All he could manage to say was ok and that their staff were shit and they couldn't get good ones. There had not been a pre stay check done on the accommodation and at $200+ per night it is definitely not what we expected.
Great clean Park. Across the road to the beach  ⛱️ easy to grab a scooter.
Great place love the fact they sell Gelati ice cream at reception the only downside was communal toilets and showers but that's ok definitely will be back 👍

We were a bit sceptical to stay here as some of the reviews were poor. However, to our surprise, we really loved the campsite. We decided to stay an extra night. The staff here were really helpful and welcoming including the maintenance team. We stayed at the powered campsite. The shower/bathroom were really clean. We felt really safe here for the few nights.

Stayed at a powered caravan site and found that the park was generally poorly maintained. There were no lights over the amenities door and without a torch it was impossible to
key in the entry code. Amenities were clean with good showers. Lots of midgies and plenty of insect repellent required.
Considered overpriced and under maintained.
Great amenities, kitchen is the best we’ve had on a campsite, good powered sites

Service was pretty good while checking in. The amenities were clean, but the staff had an interesting way of cleaning it. They would come in with a hose and spray water on every single surface. Pool was nice but small. Toilets were being cleaned, so my son used the accessible toilet. There was no handle on the inside to get out and he was stuck. My husband tried to open it but it wouldn’t work, since the door was locked with a key.We had to get the key from the office to get him out. If he didn’t have his phone, he would have been stuck there for ages. Good location, roads not good for scootering.

100% Recommend "DO NOT" stay there, especially the cabins! Everything was filthy dirty and extremely uncomfortable with no privacy! Also received a notification from reception about thieves targeting the area and seen it for myself as they were targeting our cabin and got scared off!! Exhausted from a 9 hour drive and obviously on edge due to the notification I was so uncomfortable to the point I booked elsewhere immediately and unfortunately did not get a refund.

Pictures on site are not what it seems! Very outdated and extremely unhygienic!!!
Great place to stay
We stayed in our caravan. Sites are large & easy to access. Staff are friendly & helpful. Pool area is great.

Ya he estado en varios hoteles de la compañía y muy a gusto con las instalaciones. Motel con habitación amplia barata ,equipada con cocina y baño. A las afueras de la ciudad pero accesible por el paseo marítimo.

Camping- we stayed for 4 nights in the camp ground the spot we originally had when we turned up was a bit hit or miss and we expected rain so it would have ended up as a mud pit, but spoke to reception and the lovely lady help us out and gave us a caravan spot in our rooftop tent and trailer, as we expected it rained heavy for 2 days, so we needed to use the camp kitchen that is very nice in the corner of the camp site, the only thing I would say not the parks fault but we had a few rough bogans also stay in a ground tent that ended sleeping in the camp kitchen a couple times, so it made it very uncomfortable to go in a do our thing for dinner and breakfast and another guy was re making his fishing cast net on the dining table.
The shower block was great very clean showers and toilets just not a fan off the no water urinals find they form a scum and can smell. All in all 8/10
Rooms- The room was very well laid out modern furniture and all the bits you need to have a comfortable night, unfortunately all that was let down by the dirtyness of the room, start with the black long hair up the shower wall not a little a fair few, then around the floor of the toilet and on the bed. The fridge was dirty inside and under the sofa and down the side of the sofa was filthy with old smoke butts and food.
We did see the I think the room cleaners in the buggy having a drag race that resulted in them loosing the Henry vac of the back on the road.
Take into mind we have allot of young children running around?
With a bit more care in the cleaning department it would be great but not today, the price is fair for a night, just get that cleaning sorted. All in all 6/10 (pics attached)

Stayed  a weekend in Townsville at Rowes bay caravan park in a cabin. Cabin was clean and comfortable. Shower was fantastic and the linen and pillows were perfect. The young couple who are managing the park were so very helpful and nothing was to hard and they had great knowledge of the area which is always nice when organising days trips. Will definitely be back.

Do Not make a reservation at this campground!

The uploaded photos of the cabins do not reflect what they actually look like! This was even confirmed to us by one of the employees.
The cabins are very old, small, smelly (smoke) and very dirty.

Cabins with “beach view” only offer a view to the noisy street and car park.
The cabins are way too expensive and definitely not worth the money.

The lady working at the desk is impolite, disrespectful and not helpful when having a problem or issue to solve.
We wanted to change our cabin but were told there was no other cabin available for the night - which was not true because you could have booked it online.

We stayed at a different place this night and the promised refund has been rejected.

Please safe your money and find another nice place to stay in town.

Be careful: Dont book any cabins here!!!The pictures of the cabins posted by the owner do not correspond to reality and are heavily edited. The cabins are nasty, noisy, old and smell like smoke. If I were able, I would give no stars at all. On the pictures online the cabins looked very new, fresh and shiny with direct oceanview to enjoy while sitting on the front porch. When we arrived we were given a one room queen size bed which looked very old and not well mantained. It did not at all look like the pictures. It was also ridiculous to say there is an ocean view since we were looking on the noisy highway and a parking lot with lots of cars. Behind that there is somewhere the ocean. The inside of the cabin and another alternative cabin were  terrible. There was still plastic trash below the bed, the sheets and the sofa had stains, one bed was not made, the fridge was dirty and it smelled strongly like smoke. Everything was old and not well mantained. It was not worth the high price. We did not feel comfortable to stay there and booked a different hotel last minute. The people at the reception even agreed that the pictures did not reflect the reality but never kept their promise of a refund. Stay away from that place if you dont want to waste your money. Worst experience we have even made on a campsite. Cannot believe they they still belong to BIG4 family since their standard is definitely not met.
Kitchen facilities were great, toilets always dirty. I felt the people in the campsites (mostly the long stay residents) seem to just leave their pots in the sink. Personally I wouldn't stay again.

Wir waren im Oktober 2024 bereits zum vierten Mal im BIG4 Tasman Holiday Parks - Rowes Bay (nach 2017, 2019 und 2023) und hatten wieder einen angenehmen Aufenthalt. Die Lage direkt am Wasser und in der Nähe von Townsville ist ideal und bietet eine schöne Kulisse sowie gute Möglichkeiten für Ausflüge.

Die Stellplätze sind sehr gross und gepflegt, und die Anlagen wie die Küche und die Grillbereiche sind gut ausgestattet. Es gibt zwar ein paar Dinge, die in die Jahre gekommen sind, aber insgesamt ist der Platz gut in Schuss und bietet eine komfortable Umgebung.

Dieser Platz eine solide Wahl, um schnell nach Townsville reinfahren und von dort aus Ausflüge machen zu können.
Beautiful place to stay

Sehr schöner Campingplatz
Hat sehr viele Stellplätze und ist sehr groß
Super Standort

We stayed here in 2023 and found it a lovely reasonably priced park so stayed again.||Last year, the manager helped us with overseas voting in New Zealand whilst we were on holiday.||Park is right across the road from the beach and park.||Very clean, they have done a lot to upgrade the park.||Close to the CBD.||Nice swimming pool.||Clean amenities.||Thanks once again

On our road trip from Cairns to Sydney we decided to camp 2 nights in Townsville.
My mistake!
Townsville itself was just ok.  If you have a choice, bypass the town and go further north to Mission Beach or further south to Arlie Beach.

As for the Roses Bay campground, the positive:
Nice trees on the property and the bathrooms are ok.
The negative:
Felt unsafe - signs all around the campsite saying to not leave valuables in our vehicle.
(Hard to do when your camping in your vehicle)
So many bugs.
Nasty flies and mosquitoes that came out at dawn and dusk but would only surround our campsite.
The pool had broken tiles and the water was nasty.
The wifi barely worked
The location was ok but not great.
The laundry was expensive.

In review, if I travelled the east coast again, I’d skip Townsville all together but if I had to stop, I’d select another campsite or even a parking lot before staying at this Tasman Holiday park.
As every Canadian says - Sorry.

I stayed in a cabin with 4 of my friends. We had a triple bunk bed and a full-sized bed. There was parking behind each cabin and a communal bathroom close by. The shower pressure was AMAZING! I felt so clean after. ||Quiet hours are quite early, and we had to move across the street to the beach in order to talk to my friends. It was quite warm in the cabins, but there is AC. WiFi is included.||Lots of bugs! I was sleeping on the bottom bunk where the mattress was on the floor. There were ants all over the floor. It was not the end of the world, though! The kitchen was stocked, but with only the bare minimum pots and pans.

Service was okay at check-in. Paid not really much attention. Amenities close by but outdated. The showers are too low for men >1.75m and also for women who are a bit taller. You will be able to look over the walls, bit awkward. Next to the road, bit of a nuisance and a weird feel overall at the campsite. Didn’t feel really safe. Some permanent sites or sites occupied by workers. The town and beach are close by. That is nice.

Recently stayed in an ensuited Cabin. The room was clean, fresh and well appointed with everything required for my week long stay. The reception staff were friendly and helpful. Would definitely stay again.

Nice area, plenty of space & activities for primary school age.
High price though.
Great location
Wonderful caravan park,great atmosphere,scenery,clean amenities,close to town n helpful staff😊

Overall, the campsite was clean, with a nice kitchen area and close to the beach and city. However, the BBQ and shower ran out of gas during our two-night stay, and there were too many sand flies, which was a bit bothersome. The price is fair for the amenities, but a few small improvements would make it better. Stricter camp rules would be nice
We stayed here for 4 nights. Toilets were never cleaned. Most of them with skid marks. Expensive site, full of midges.

My husband booked and paid a deposit online in June for a 2 day stay in August,  the price was $62 maybe $64 per night for a premium powered site, this was the most expensive site cost on our 5 week Queensland holiday.
We arrived and checked in, however the next day while checking our bank account, we realised we had been overcharged. My husband went to reception to get the problem rectified, however he was told the cost of the site was $90 per night!!!! ( This was mid week and not peak season) He asked to speak to the manager who confirmed the cost and would not honour the original price we booked at. Even with our Big 4 membership discount the price is totally inflated. Not only is this morally wrong , it's probably illegal.
We  left, after our stay, frustrated and totally covered with midge bites (they were all through the artificial grass ). Bathrooms were grubby also. We will not be back.

Fairly basic park and well over priced. It's quite a bit more expensive than the coconut in Cairns which is an amazing park. The location is good for access to town. You can go across the road to the walking track then walk/ride into the strand area. There's a few park's along Rowe's Bay that are good for the kid's. The park inside caravan park isn't very good. It does have a descent pool though

We came and stayed in one of the onsuite cabins, air conditioner and fan, and we really enjoyed staying there, the pool was great and BBQ area, right across the road from the beach, close to town it was everything we wanted so really enjoyed our stay

Way too expensive, $100 per night on a powered site. Dearest in Australia..
As well $100 a night to be eaten by Midge is ridiculous.
Only one out of four BBQs was working. Expensive for what you get.
Couldn't swing a cat on our site as photos show. Very expensive for very little room or enjoyment.  Such a shame.

Lovely setting but outrageously overpriced for what you expect. Hot water out during a cold snap. Apologies not good enough. No security. Homeless public use of showers and facilities at night is unsafe and unhygienic. Very unsatisfactory

Nice little place, very busy and lots for the kids to do/explore. The location is perfect, right across from the beach and not to far from The Strand. Unfortunately our little 2 bedroom cabin wasn't very clean, there were quite a few dishes/crockery etc that were dirty in the cupboards and ants all over the bench. Other then that it was okay.

We were there after check-in time and got one of the last spots. The spot was crooked and opposite was a permanent camper (didn't look particularly well-maintained). The power connection was very far away, so we thought we were on an unpowered site. The toilets and showers, however, were new and clean. The camp kitchen was also clean. Absolutely not good value for money.
Great cabin for 2 people. Across the road from the water. Clean and well equipped. Great service and amenities.
It's OK

Great position right on the foreshore. Easy to get up early and go for the morning walk. Plenty of space to park the caravan. Well we have come back again to this great caravan park. Now, there are sign every where saying lock it or lose it. As we did and glad we did as someone tried to break in via our screen door. See the photo below. It won't stop us coming back either
Super friendly reception staff! Went out of their way to help us out. Great caravan sites. Love the diagonal layout. Makes it easy to reverse caravan into. Clean amenities, too.

The BIG4 Tasman Holiday Parks Rowes Bay caravan park is a large resort style park on the shorefront of Rowes Bay and close to the well known Strand district of Townsville.

The great location of this park is, without doubt, the greatest feature beyond the usual resort style amenities (pool, playground, etc). The park is almost “absolute waterfront” with only a road separating it from the waterfront. There’s a wonderful park across the road and Townsville’s famous Strand is only a short drive away, as is the CBD and Castle Hill which overlooks the park.

Majority of powered sites have a 45 degree, angled spot for your caravan providing an easier reverse into your spot but also giving you a sense that your site is larger than what it actually is.

While the amenities block could do with a refresh it still has everything you need. When it comes to choice within Townsville, I’d say this is definitely the place to go because there isn’t a lot on offer otherwise.
Friendly and welcoming staff, close to all services. We enjoyed a good site, well established and quiet - other than the occasional jet plane🤔. We enjoyed our stay 👍.

Another awesome park close to things. The staff on arrival were absolutely fantastic very friendly and professional great sites and very clean facilities. Great walking path along the water front with a fantastic view of Magnetic Island.

We’ve been travelling in our van for the last 5 weeks up the coast so have been to a lot of caravan parks. Didn’t love this park, the park could do with a bit of TLC, gardens looked very dry and barren. Amenities were also run down. There were a lot of signs around about theft so this must be an issue there and it just didn’t have a great vibe. The 3 stars were for the location which was great. Close to town and across from a lovely walkway and the ocean.

An diesem Ort wurden wir sehr sehr herzlich empfangen, wir haben uns von Anfang an sehr wohl gefühlt.  Auf die Anfrage einen ruhigeren Stellplatz für unser Wohnmobil zu bekommen durfte ich mir einen auswählen. Der absolut ruhig war. Die Duschen und Wc’s waren immer einwandfrei. Und auch Tipps für die Umgebung waren klasse Wir kommen gern wieder. Lieben Dank.

Great place to stay
Fresh and clean cabins
Beautiful view of the ocean
Staff were very accommodating.
Perfect location.

Super comfy stay. Staff at reception were amazing, especially when we arrived to find out we had booked the wrong cabin. The park was really full as it was a Saturday but the amazing staff assisted in upgrading us to a cabin to suit the family with ensuite etc.
Friendly staff, clean cabin and a view of the ocean, absolutely no complaints here!

Don't go there at all! The manager is an aggressive woman who treated us violently verbally. Days after paying for the site, she randomly added $80 to it (according to her due to a system error) and demanded that we pay it.  She insulted us, pointed her finger at us while yelling and then kicked us out. From day one she was passive aggressive with us and also with other camp clients.

The site I stayed in was a dirt slab with sharp rocks and twigs all over the ground. No working wifi and a closed amenities black meaning a long walk to the next ones in the middle of the night to share 2 toilets with the entire park that looked like they hadn’t been cleaned in days!
On top of that I received the worst customer service I have ever experienced this morning. I was  spoken to appallingly by the plus sized lady with brown hair and hand tattoos at the reception. I went in to request a second night extension due to a late return ferry from magnetic island. She had zero urgency to help with my inquiry and was instead fixated on something with the printer for 10 minutes with no communication towards my inquiry. When I explained I had a ferry to catch and if someone else could help me she was rude and blunt and said no I’m looking at it. After some back and forth with rude attitude from the woman she said that there’s no availability even though i could see plenty on line so that was clearly a lie…
I left the office and after reflecting on the disgusting communication I received from her I went back in to lodge a compliant. As I walked back into the reception 15 minutes later I hear her mocking me to her co worker ….
After asking for her name she replied “ I don’t have to do anything I can just tell you any name” Her co worker in the wheel chair said “just give her your name” to which she replied “it’s ren”
I asked to speak to the manager which she said no but and said “you can just leave”
I am absolutely appalled by her attitude and sarcasm towards a paying customer and would never stay in this park again. I recommend anyone wanting to stay in Townsville to choose another park!!!
Poor service. Won't answer phone. Want all done online which is ok until you have trouble. Need to talk to human to sort things out, 😕.
3 days of chill time, before heading home

We booked a powered tent site and ended up on a site with no shade and very dry and spiky grass. We asked if we could get another one but the lady at the reception told us everything else is already booked although we saw a lot of free sites during our 2 nights stay. We couldn't see the power point at first and asked at the reception, but both employees were not able to tell us more, only that our site is powered. It turned out to be at the other end of our neighbor's site, who was fortunately very nice and lended us 2 extension leads to add to our extension lead - this was the only way we could access the power.
The toilets and showers were okay but quite dirty in the morning. The kitchen was quite new and very nice.
The view of Magnetic Island in front of the park was really nice.
All in all, it's not a terrible park but too expensive for what you get. We stayed at way better Tasman parks for a similar price.
Really good campsite ! Thank you so much !

Nous avons passé quelques nuits dans une cabine sans toilette. La cabine était propre, draps propres. Par contre impossible de dormir avec la clim qui fait le bruit d'un camion et qui fait trembler la cabine. Le lit double est trop petit pour 2 personnes, mon conjoint est tombé du lit et le matelas est un vrai trampoline. Les couvertures ne sentaient pas le propre. Et ensuite la cuisine est envahie de fourmis. La wifi est éteinte la nuit.
Les sanitaires étaient propres. Le camping est au bord de mer.
Nicht so sauber wie in Airlie Beach, beide Barbecue in der Nähe ohne Funktion

What a fantastic caravan park. Nikki’s service was exceptional but all the staff we encountered were friendly too. We stayed one night in a powered site and then took respite in an ensuite cabin for the second night. The shared amenities were clean, showers had good pressure and quickly hot. There was also a swimming pool and small tennis court which was unique. We did not use the camp kitchen but when walking by they looked well maintained and modern. The location is stellar, particularly as you can get anywhere in Townsville in 15mins - Neuron and Beam scooters were often available outside which was convenient. I would definitely stay again when in Townsville.

Would love to leave a better view, but I can't.   The park is well located,  the staff are amazing, the pool is wonderful and there are some lovely facilities around the park.  We stayed in a cabin that slept 4, we are a couple thankfully as there was insufficient equipment in the kitchen for 4.   The cutlery drawer was filthy and there was dried on food on the cutlery.   The kitchen bench had holes burned in it.  We got a fry pan which was too big for the element, one small pot with no lid, a colander, grater and salad bowl.  The bathroom was disgusting.   The toilet seat is delaminating and utterly unsanitary.   The shower head delivers a hose pipe like stream with a few little sprays around it.  We got a bit chilly in the night with the air-conditioning so got a blanket out of the cupboard.  It was tacky to touch and was obviously not clean and fresh. We went and bought our own the next day.
In an area where the biting insects abound we could not believe the screen door was just proped up against the window unable to be used

Vi boede på en unpowered campsite.pladsen var dejlig stor. ||Køkkenet car helt nyt og lækkert. Det er indendørs og har både køleskab, ovn og komfur. Der var også plads til at sidde både ude og inde at spise. ||Parken lignende at den trængte til en lille kærlig hånd, ift. vejene, bedene osv. Men det var stadig rigtig hyggeligt at være der. ||Badeværelserne var fine, dog havde jeg lidt problemer med at få koden til at virke til dametoilettet og endte ved toiletterne ved poolen, som virkelig trængte til at kærlig hånd og opgradering. ||Det var rimelig gennemsnitlig park og fin til en nat eller to.

Nous sommes restés deux nuits dans ce camping, le nettoyage n’a jamais était fait dans les toilettes et douches. Il n’y avait pas de papier toilette non plus !
La cuisine qui normalement ouvre à 6 am à ouvert seulement à 8:30 am, inadmissible quand nos affaires pour cuisiner sont à l’intérieur.
De plus nous avons demandé des photocopie à la la réception, pas aimable du tout et n’a pas eu envie de nous aider, alors qu’on allait payer.
Pour avoir fait plusieurs big4 celui ci est le pire.

Nous sommes restés 2 nuits à cet endroit qui est bien situé dans la ville. Nous avions loué la cabine sans toilette. Pour aller au toilette commune, il faut un code pour débarrer la porte et malheureusement, 1fois sur 4, le code ne fonctionnait pas. Avec des enfants, vraiment pas pratique. Il y avait vraiment énormément de fourmis dans l'appartement. Le divan et un des draps contour étaient tachés à notre arrivée. L'accueil à notre arrivée était bien, mais le lendemain, c'était quelqu'un d'autre qui soupirait énormément devant nous pour peu importe la raison. Ne fait plus partie de la bannière Big 4.

Bumpy roads on the way in, bad sanitary facilities (apparently no cleaning on sundays, no toilet paper, shower was cold for minutes), power didn't work on our powered motorhome site, no sun shades over playground and pool.
Summary: way too expensive for what it is, we've been to much better sites for less.
Good holiday park. Amenities were very good and clean! Kitchen was inside, air-conditioned and very well occupied!

Deb at the desk was very rude and unhelpful. The prices are overpriced and the camping spots are lackluster to say the least. Old toilet facilities and no actual working WiFi. Small pool, and overall run down.

Would not recommend, go to one of the many other parks around Townsville.

NIE WIEDER!

Die Plätze sind überhaupt nicht gepflägt, die meisten Plätze sind schräg!

Wir bekamen einen platz und uns wurde gesagt, wenn er uns nicht gefällt dann könnten wir ihn wechseln.
Zuerst hatten wir einen sonnigen Platz mit nur wenig Schatten. Wir gingen an die Rezeption und fragten nach einem schattigen. Jaa wir bekamen einen neuen  aber der war noch schlimmer als der 1.. Komplett sonne den ganzen Tag bei über 30 Grad.

Für Leute die angewiesen auf Internet sind, es funktioniert nicht! Sehrschade!

We have spent 3 months in total here and really enjoyed it! The common areas were cleaned daily, everything very neat. The reception ladies were always very helpful. Nikki (manager) has been so lovely with us.

We loved the location as it is right in front of the beach and only a short drive to the city / shopping centre. Best campsite/holiday park we have stayed in during our travels!

We always stay here when we are in Townsville.
The team are amazing. Always look after we us.
Clean park, facilities,  amenities cabins and villas .
Recommend booking direct rather than through another service provider.
Second time ive stayed here...excellent park..great staff

I cannot express my gratefulness enough to the ladies at reception for the exceptional job they do. Very kind & incredibly willing to help as much as possible (especially when you book the wrong cabin, silly me).  Cabin was gorgeous, and very clean. Pool was clean & easily accessible. Location is right on the beach perfect for daily walks. Thank you Tasman HP for having me I will be back in the future!!
Had the best stay in the cabins very clean an tiny. Very Lovely stuff, a big thank you to Nikki for your hospitality also great location with lots of sea breeze

Really shouldn't vouch for the cabins, even though they look amazing, we're in a camper van. This place is immaculate, the bathrooms are spotless, the laundry room is clean and efficient. We'll appointed amenities, pool and even a tennis court. Just across the road from the sea. Apparently you can walk into town if you have a spare 40 minutes. I'd come again

The ladies were very helpful and chatty from the moment we checked in. The place is very clean, however the shower blocks are a little dated, but having said that they are by no way in bad condition. The location was good for us, we used e scooters to head along into town but it was only a 15 - 30 minute walk anyways. (We took the scenic route along the coast and up to kissing Point for a spectacular and historic view). We would love to come back and explore more as we only had 2 nights here.

Vi måtte bede om at flytte plads 2 gange efter ankomst. Vi var 5 så det krævede lidt mere plads til bord og stole end vi havde booket. Men selvom receptionen var fuld og der var travlt løste de det med et smil|Pladsen er ren og velholdt og henvender sig både til familier og par. Skønt sted!

Have camped here before, but got a studio cabin on a walk in basis this time, as we were desperate for air con. Cabin was perfect for couple, very clean and near new, by the pool. We were even able to park our large car and boat right beside the cabin. A little expensive for a studio but made up for with quality and comfort. Pool was (almost) refreshing in the tropical New Year heat, but tiles need a clean above water line. Staff were great. Will definitely book again.
Friendly staff, I was upgraded from a standard cabin to a newly renovated 3 bedroom cabin at no extra charge. So grateful and made our weekend. Thank you so much.
Great park large sites friendly staff

Beds were only made with bottom sheets. Had one toilet roll in bathroom in the cupboard nothing on the roll. Aircon wasn’t cold . Had stayed here previously but under new management was totally different to our previous stay

Fantastic little place.
Family of 5 and we have really enjoyed our stay.
Rooms are a bit squashy but also much cheaper than most motels.
Pool is nice - water is clean and well kept but pool tiles desperately need scrubbing above the water line.
Prime position! Quiet(ish) street and so close to the beach.
We will definitely stay here again!

This the WORST caravan park I have EVER been to -

- Ground maintenance - non-existent, massive leaf LITTER collection on the ground, ALL park roads and caravan parking bays (only collected if guests swept leaves up into an obvious pile), palm fronds left out onto the road to force collection, bats everywhere, sap dropping from trees and bat poo all over cars.
- Older poorly maintained toilet blocks, toilets often blocked along with sinks, pin number locks often failing and not allowing toilet entry despite the being correct.
- MASSIVE issues with mossies and Midges, you must be lathered on mossie spray all day and feel sticky and revolting.
- PROMISED a cheaper park (temporary storage) and reneged (no booking?) when ready to move the van even though discussed with senior staff, NOT asked to book, ULTIMATELY EXTREMELY expensive at $15 per day / $450 per 30 days, storage found for $98.00 per month with 24-hour access gate number.
- Dump point REGULARLY blocked, full to the top with effluent, several hours to be cleared, van poo tanks stay full and unusable.
- HORRIBLE quality drinking water …. a good quality filter is a MUST, water quality no doubt dating back to when it was a municipal park (?? 1970’s to recently).
- The park STILL has numerous lifelong permanents that bring the overall appearance down very notably.

Do yourself a FAVOUR don’t bother going to Tasman Holiday Parks Rowes Bay.
Very close to all amenities and Very convenient

Unpowered tents sites are extremely unfriendly. The ground beneath the campsite is all rocky, and the tent pegs are all broken. Moreover, despite plenty of empty spots, they crowded all the guests together, which is really annoying.
However the kitchen have AC and clean ,so not too bad
Campers van

Location is good. Campground within walking distance to The Strand. Busstation in front of the entrance with service to the Breakwater Ferry Terminal.

Do avoid the evergreen sites. Nothing premium about it. Spaces are tight and there is no shade on most spots.
Millions of sandflies in the mornings and evenings.

Showers and toilets a bit dated but fine.

All in all rather average. Would only return for the location and if only intending to sleep there. Not a place where I would want to spend my day.

Stayed in our Caravan in July. Damage was done to the van. Admin was notified immediately.  A report was written up about the incident. We have since had the the van repaired and forwarded the bill to admin. Still waiting for compensation or even a follow up phone call. Very poor service from Admin.
Amazing view.  Nikki is extremely helpful would recommend calling her direct.  Cabins are beautiful and there’s lots to do for families will definitely be coming back

Last time I stay out the park I didn't have a very good experience.......

BUT THIS TIME I STAY ABOUT 2 WEEKS AND I CANT SAY ANYTHING BAD BECUSE WE HAVE BEEN LIOK AFTER BYE NIKKI AND SHE AMAZING WE ARE LOOKING FORWARD ON COMING BACK IN JANUARY

AVOID! There is sandflies everywhere. We got bitten so badly during our stay on the artificial grass campsite that we had to go see a doctor. And yes - we saw the flies every morning and evening, so it was definitely on the campground.
The campground itself is totally fine, but a bit overpriced for what you get. We stayed in better Tasman parks, but also in worse.
Great place!
They put us right next to the dump point even though there were a lot of free sites, site was uneven, amenities were old but clean.

What a wonderful place with very helpful and happy staff! I was stuck for a caravan site at 5:30pm on Saturday and called the park. Nikki the site manager was so kind and calming, then artanged a fantastic spot for me and my two dogs. I couldn't be happier with this park.
Always great. My go to park. a great place to stay.

Wir haben im September diesen Jahres zwei Nächte auf diesem Campground verbracht, welchen wir schon von unseren Reisen in den Jahren 2017 und 2019 kannten. Die Powered Sites sind gut gelegen und bieten genügend Abstand zueinander. Achtung: Ein grosser Teil der Plätze hat keine Bäume, sodass man der prallen Sonne ausgesetzt ist. Die sanitären Anlagen sind in die Jahre gekommen, werden jedoch täglich sauber gereinigt. Wir würden bei einer nächsten Durchreise durchaus wieder auf dieser Anlage Halt machen.

The location is excellent you can easily walk through the park and over to The Strand. It's very quiet at night and the park itself is nice. Staff here are great, received great service from Brittany and anyone else we interacted with who worked for the park. We had a spot in the "evergreen" area so not tonnes of shade, sites a bit sloped but otherwise fine.

There are several amenities blocks, the one by the pool seems well used and wasn't as clean as the others... don't look at the fan by the door in the ladies. The kitchen areas are actually quite nice. The main kitchen/TV  is well set up with two cooks tops, ovens, etc. The smaller outdoor kitchens downside, other campers left dirty bowls, piles of dishes and spills everywhere so unless a staff member was constantly there, it would be impossible to keep clean. An extra cook top here would benefit as there is only one 4 burner set up and it's very popular so always a wait. Had issues with the BBQ in the outdoor kitchen and by the pool but seems they were fixed the next day.

It's well set up if you have kids and maybe better when not so busy.

I stayed there 1 month and I can only recommend this place !
It’s a clean, quiet and beautiful site with a great view in front the beach.
The best living room and kitchen area I’ve ever seen.
I want to thanks all of the staff members for their kindness and especially Nikki :)
The best caravan park in the world !!
It was clean tidy,friendly, and the food was superb.

We had a 2BR Deluxe Cabin for our stay with our adult son. Everything worked well and the beds were comfy, all bathroom and kitchen amenities worked very well. The cabin could do with an internal paint as there was a lot of wear and tear. Curtain over the sliding door needs more rings and cabin needs a srping clean in many places especially above the sliding door and webs above aircon unints. Pool an grounds are great. Location supberb !

Good location
Good pool
Friendly staff
Nice ammenities
Bad sandflies
What a hidden gem! Waterfront, clean, tidy and genuinely helpful/accomodating staff at an affordable price. Thank you so much we will definitely be back again.

Fabulous stay! We stayed in the accessible 3 bedroom cabin.
So much room - functional kitchen, big bathroom, great size rooms with a beautiful view of the park and ocean. Great value, clean - highly recommend!

Caravan sites are fantastic. Best spot we've stayed in so far on this QLD coastal trip the past 5 weeks. Well laid out. Good facilities. Superb location. Cycle track and beach right outside your door. Definitely stay here again.

Lovely, peaceful, location.Clean sites and amenities. Handy cycle or walk to city strand along coastal paths and boardwalk. Great camp kitchen apart from both BBQs running out of gas at same time and bright lights that you may need to turn off yourself after 9:30pm. Midges morning and evening moved us south earlier than planned....

What a great holiday park, definitely one of the top 3 we have ever stayed at. Plenty of space for larger campervans and the installation of artificial turf is just terrific as most grassy areas in other high-use parks just quickly degenerate into dusty messes (or quagmires after rain). Very helpful staff and fabulous location top off this gold standard park!
Perfect for a quick getaway with the kids. The beach is a great spot to spend during sunset right before dinner out and about along the Strand close by.

The value here is in the location, not the comfort. The cabins are definitely not suitable for more than one adult (or couple) to share. Bunks are stacked three-high, but under a normal height ceiling. Makes it very difficult to get into or out of.
Also, there were ants - alive and dead - on top of the bedding in the bunks. The dead ones indicate that staff knew they were an issue, the alive ones indicate that the issue wasn’t rectified.

Stayed here for a couple weeks after backpacking around, best camper Park in townsville, super clean, loads to do like tennis, giant chess, swimming pool and free WiFi 🙌

Awesome showers and facilities and a fantastic kitchen if you're camping. Best I've been to in a long while, would recommend.

The manager is a super-star, above and beyond, champion of gold 👏 👌

Good spot close to the water shops and not far from the strand. An easy walk into the eateries and the shopping precinct. The park also provided entertainment and a free sausage sizzle last night for all the guests 😀. Well worth checking out and a great initiative by the park manager 👍

The Good, friendly helpful staff, large indoor camp-kitchen with TV if you like that , we don't.  Small outdoor camp kitchen almost finished very nice. Sites tight, but we moved to small unpowered site on astro turf, which was good. Directly across road from beach and good walking path. Good birdwatching nearby.
The Bad, we got smashed by sand flies, I got over 50 bites, no warning from staff on arrival. I got locked out of mens amenities in middle of night, the lock is faulty. Smallish plain pool for location.
Not on my favourites list

Park could use some development - it appears this is in progress as "evergreen" sites with artificial turf appear to have been put in place recently. Other sites are unevenly grassed meaning dirt and sticks and mud when it rains. Amenity lock malfunctioning during my stay meant one block unavailable for some time. Appears well maintained overall. Good pool and other facilities.  It's a good walk to and around "Kissing Point" to "The Strand" but may be a little too far if you're older.
Worst service here, receptionist really not nice !!!!
Camp sites......A1.....camp kitchen, superb, location-amazing. Service wonderful.....
Not sure was outside
Always stay here. Great location, friendly staff.
Great site and staff

I went to see a friend and was surprised how nice the park is.
There are lots of trees between the sites offering shade and some privacy.
I will definitely be recommending this place to friends who are looking for somewhere to camp.
The best caravan Park in Australia.  ! Great position ! Great service from Nikki & the team.. Upgrades happening all the time. Love it!!!
